_description: Model derivations for Australia vs Egypt 2026-07-03 R32

elo.flag: Elo strongly contradicts live market + current-tournament form. Not trusted -- market wins per RULE1/RULE3.

rule_checks.RULE1: avg(market consensus) used as primary source -- no crowd data available pre-submission for this match
rule_checks.RULE7: INACTIVE -- Australia win=25.64% >= 15% threshold, not a heavy-favorite blowout scenario
rule_checks.RULE8: FIRES -- Draw=33.78% >= 25%. Competitive/draw-heavy match; comparison-type and thin-sample model estimates shaded toward 0.50
rule_checks.RULE9: Applies to Trezeguet (no reliable liquid market baseline beyond the illiquid scorer price; used positional/role prior: impact substitute in group stage, R32 question implies probable starter)
rule_checks.RULE12: Q13 (offsides) flagged: own-team-only average (lambda=0.667) vs match-environment average (lambda=2.0) differ by 6x due to n=3 thin sample on each side. Blended toward the higher, more historically-consistent match-environment reading given prior precedent (MEX-ECU used match-environment-style lambda=2.0 successfully).
rule_checks.RULE15: Not applicable -- Australia not a blowout underdog (25.64% > 10% floor for Scenario A)

espn_insight.note_on_aus_form: Australia's group-stage output is thin (season-high 12 shots) and their one win (2-0 vs Turkey) came against the run of play (28% possession). Egypt were the better team by shots/possession in every one of their 3 group games.
espn_insight.note_on_trezeguet: Trezeguet did not start any group game; single sub appearance (76' vs NZL). Market price of ~21% for a game he may or may not start already reflects this uncertainty.
